name: Santa María de Cambre - loop via Monte Xalo
name_original: Santa María de Cambre - bucle por Monte Xalo
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cambre traverse a diverse landscape in Galicia, Spain, offering a range of experiences. The area features varying terrains, from the peaceful Galician countryside to coastal stretches with sea views. Routes often include significant elevation changes, with some paths leading through protected areas like a Biosphere Reserve. The region provides both well-paved surfaces and some segments that can be unpaved, catering to different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Cambre The most popular road cycling route is Santa María de Cambre - loop via Monte Xalo, a 24.1 miles (38.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes ascents via Monte Xalo. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Abegondo - Cecebre - Biosphere Reserve - figure of 8 loop, a difficult 54.0 miles (86.8 km) path. This route navigates through the Biosphere Reserve and demands very good fitness. Local road cyclists also love the Mera Beach – Sada Waterfront and Marina loop from Cecebre, a 28.8 miles (46.3 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Cambre is defined by varied terrains, coastal views, and the Galician countryside. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, accommodating different ability levels. The routes in Cambre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cambre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes can I find around Cambre?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes available around Cambre, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists of all levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Cambre?
A: Cambre's road cycling routes traverse a varied landscape, from the peaceful Galician countryside to coastal stretches with sea views. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, with some paths leading through protected areas. While most surfaces are well-paved, some segments might be unpaved, catering to different preferences.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Cambre offers several easier road cycling routes. While the majority are moderate to difficult, there are 3 routes classified as easy. These typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, perfect for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the Cambre area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Cambre has over 30 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971550975/abegondo-cecebre-biosphere-reserve-figure-of-8-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abegondo - Cecebre - Biosphere Reserve - figure of 8 loop&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 86.8 km path that navigates through the Biosphere Reserve and requires very good fitness due to its substantial elevation gain.

Q: Can I find routes that offer coastal views?
A: Absolutely. Several routes in the Cambre area extend towards the coast, providing beautiful sea views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24673414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mera Beach – Sada Waterfront and Marina loop from Cecebre&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a 46.3 km ride through coastal areas.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions or landmarks to see while cycling?
A: Yes, the routes often pass by interesting natural and historical sites. You can cycle near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5223936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cecebre Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside Beach at Cecebre Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also lead through the Biosphere Reserve, showcasing the region's natural beauty. Additionally, ancient Romanesque churches in Cambre itself add cultural appeal.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Cambre?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Cambre are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971582266/santa-maria-de-cambre-loop-via-monte-xalo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Cambre - loop via Monte Xalo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31954664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peiraio Mill loop from Cecebre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Cambre?
A: Galicia generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures and less rainfall, making them ideal for road cycling. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially for coastal routes, though it can be warmer.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Cambre?
A: While specific parking facilities for cyclists aren't detailed, Cambre, as a municipality, offers various public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking regulations and availability near your chosen route's starting point.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Cambre?
A: For families, focusing on the easier and shorter routes is recommended. These routes often feature less traffic and gentler gradients, providing a safer and more enjoyable experience for all ages. Look for routes with minimal elevation gain and well-paved surfaces.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Cambre?
A: The road cycling routes in Cambre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrains, the blend of countryside and coastal views, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Where can I find historical sites or castles along the routes?
A: Several routes might bring you close to historical sites. For instance, you can find castles and historical gardens in the wider region. Consider exploring routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3532496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Antón&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3093759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Carlos Garden&lt;/a&gt;, which offer glimpses into the area's rich history.

Q: Are there any routes that connect Cambre to nearby cities like A Coruña?
A: Yes, there are routes that connect Cambre to nearby urban centers. A route spanning just over 16 km connects Cambre to A Coruña, primarily on paved surfaces with a mix of cycleways and quiet roads. This offers an introduction to the Galician countryside and the vibrant city, with attractions like the Tower of Hercules.
tips: At the end of the ascent, the path passes along the southern slope of Mount Xaló to provide a panoramic view. The top, at 514 meters above sea level, is accessible by gravel roads. From this point you descend towards Tabeaio

The church of Santa María de Cambre was referenced for the first time in the year 932, the current façade dates from the 12th century. Surrounded by beautiful gardens, it is a good place to start any walk
